title: build: add support for `--inherit-labels`
body:
Allows users to specify if they want to inherit labels from base image or not.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I see this problem is also reported in past for docker: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/52319093/how-to-not-inherit-labels-from-base-images-in-docker
Okay, between this and `--unsetenv`, we really need to get some consistency around what we provided for changing the environment/labels/annotations/ports/volumes at the command line.

Just continuing our discussion from downstream here so other maintainers can chip in as well. 

From what i can see with `--unsetenv` it deals with only single variable per command, it does not covers a use-case where user wants to clear all `ENV` vars from the base image.  That would need `--inherit-env=false` to be implemented.

Here `--inherit-labels` solves a use-case where users don't want to inherit anything from the base image. For parity with `--unsetenv` i think we will need to implement `--unsetlabel` ( which solves a different use-case IMO ).

So I think for more intuitive UX and to cover both of these problems and maintain consistency, we will need to implement
* `--inherit-labels` and `--inherit-envs` : for both `env` and `labels`.
* `--unsetlabels` and `--unsetenv`: for both `env` and `labels`.

@containers/buildah-maintainers please provide your opinions on this one.
fwiw i think immediate need is for `--unsetlabel` so i'll tweak this PR and we can talk about `--inherit-labels` later whenever its needed.
Would `--unsetlabel` be added multiple times, once for each label being removed? Or does it support being passed a list of labels to unset?
